title: Analyst- Procurement
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Job Title: Procurement Analyst - Government and Public Services Enabling Areas (GPS EA)

The Team:

As part of the GPS EA, team members work collaboratively to ensure that the organization is operating effectively and efficiently, and that it is meeting its goals and objectives. This includes developing and implementing strategic plans, managing budgets and resources, supply chain insights, supply chain projections and analysis, optimizing supply chain processes, ensuring compliance with government regulations and organizational policies, and supporting other departments in their work. The GPS EA plays a crucial role in enabling an organization to deliver high-quality services to its government and public sector clients.

Work you will do.


Support critical and complex competitive bidding processes, conducting routine
Price and cost analyses to ensure optimal outcomes.
Apply proven sourcing strategies to effectively support business clients’ requirements for software,


hardware, and other commodities.


Provide supply chain insights, analysis, and help in developing strategic supply chain approach
Provide proposal support to GPS Deloitte Proposal Teams, including advising on the development


of bills of materials for software, hardware, and other commodities as needed.


Demonstrate advanced interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills, with a strong emphasis


on delivering customer-oriented services.


Strategically manage supplier relationships to maximize value and mitigate risk.
Monitor supply markets in key categories, maintain awareness of supplier diversity programs,


and stay informed on trends within industry associations and the diverse supplier community.


Respond to field inquiries from suppliers and connect them with appropriate internal stakeholders.
Perform other job-related duties as assigned to support organizational objectives.


Qualification & Skills:


Any graduate/post-graduate with supply chain, procurement and logistics background.
Proven experience in purchasing software, SaaS solutions, and other IT-related products.
Experience in providing supply chain insights, projections and data analysis
Familiarity with the procurement portals /Procure to Pay platform is preferred
1-4 years of P2P experience in buying software and hardware.
Demonstrated expertise in negotiating and leveraging value-added reseller agreements.
Proficiency in MS Outlook, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Strong consultative and communication skills.
Ability to thrive in a challenging and dynamic environment.
Knowledge of SharePoint, Power BI, data analytics, and AI tools, including their practical applications.
Track record of effective collaboration and teamwork.
Understanding of government regulations (FAR and DFAR) is a plus


Work Location: Hyderabad

Timings: 2PM-11PM

Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

title: Associate
description:
Line of Service
Advisory



Industry/Sector
Not Applicable



Specialism
SAP



Management Level
Associate



Job Description & Summary
A career within SAP Consulting services, will provide you with the opportunity to help our clients maximise the value of their SAP investment with offerings that address sales, finance, supply chain, engineering, and human capital. We provide comprehensive consulting, system integration and implementation services across multiple SAP applications, products and technologies. Simply put, we focus on delivering business led, technology enabled change for our clients including industry specific enterprise resource planning and the latest in mobile, analytics and cloud solutions.


Minimum qualifications:



Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related technical field.
Experience in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI), SAP Process Orchestration (PO), Web Services (RESTful/SOAP) and Groovy/Java/J2EE and/or ABAP.
Experience implementing, configuring and customizing SAP adapters, User-Defined Functions and mapping routines.
Expertise in design, build, testing and troubleshooting integrations built using SAP CPI.


Preferred qualifications:


Experience with one or more of the following technologies: SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C), SAP Hybris Commerce, SAP SuccessFactors.
Experience in developing and supporting interfaces and configuring standard integrations between SuccessFactors, C4C, Salesforce, Hybris/Commerce Cloud and SAP.
Experience in integrating with third-party, on-premise and cloud-based enterprise applications.
Performance tuning experience in SAP Integrations.
Experience in full software development lifecycle (analyze, design, build, test, implement, support).
Understanding of information management, data modeling, system integration, development methodologies (including unit testing) and web technologies.
Familiarity in SAP architecture within S4/HANA landscape.



Experience and Key Competencies:


Must have 3 to 6 years of working experience in SAP Integrations and minimum 2 years of experience in SAP Cloud Platform Integration
Excellent customer facing communication skills.
Ability to translate customer’s functional requirements into a technical solution.
Ability to design, develop, document and transition enterprise solutions utilizing design patterns, reference architectures and best practices.
Independent judgement and problem-solving skills to solve highly complex integrations in a hybrid environment of on-premise, cloud and external partner systems.
Expertise in handling integrations using SFSF, ODATA, SFTP, IDOC, SOAP, HTTP, Process Direct, REST Adapters.
Must have a working understanding of security and encryption standards such as OAUTH, SSH, TLS, SSL, PGP etc.
Should be flexible to work in shifts and if necessary, extend support during weekends.




Mandatory Skill Set-SAP CPI
Preferred Skill Set-SAP CPI
Year of experience required-2-4y
Qualifications- BE/BTech/ME/MTech/MBA/MCA/ any 2 yrs fulltime regular PG degrees. Required Skills
SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)

Optional Skills

title: Analyst- Strategy & Insights-Market Research & Analysis- Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Job Title: Analyst

The Team: GPS | Strategy & Insights (S&I)

The team

The GPS Strategy & Insights (S&I) team in Government & Public Services Enterprise Solutions fulfills essential functions in support of the practice. S&I is responsible for designing & executing growth strategies and market sensing activities for Deloitte’s Federal, State & Local, and Higher Education work. This includes bespoke strategy services for internal business customers, growth forecasting, evaluation of market positioning and sizing, competitive intelligence, disruptive trends evaluations, and investment business case development.

Job Summary

The Analyst is a dedicated position, supporting Innovation & Technology/assets portfolio within S&I full time. The Analyst leverages market expertise to lead and support strategy and research initiatives across the team.

Work you’ll do


Lead or support strategic engagements, market research and market sensing analysis, and priority projects and deliver high quality and insightful deliverables to senior leaders across the practice.
Conduct comprehensive market research to support assets within the GPS Innovation & Technology portfolio
Utilize analytical techniques and research tools to synthesize data from various sources, identify patterns and trends, and generate actionable insights that support Deloitte’s business decisions and strategies.
Translate numbers, market/industry trends, and data to consumable insights - both explanatory and exploratory.
Develop processes, models, and tools to inform GPS leaders and support overall growth.
Conduct financial analysis to assess the performance, profitability, and growth of the business, using metrics and KPIs such as revenue, expenses, margins, and ROI.
Examine data based on GPS finance KFIs (Key Financial Indicators) that align with the business objectives and goals, such as, revenue, forecast, budgeting, and acquisition cost.
Develop clear and compelling reports and dashboards that visualize data and insights in a user-friendly and intuitive way, using best practices and standards for data presentation and visualization.
Provide “so what” analysis that goes beyond describing what the data shows and explains why it matters, what are the implications, and what are the recommendations for action to the senior leadership.
Cultivate connections across the practice and continually bring new insights and perspectives into analysis efforts.
Mentor team members and wider practice as applicable to increase market knowledge and use of tools and resources.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, Economics, Finance, or another similar technical discipline.
3-7 years of experience in market research and analysis, competitive intelligence, or similar strategic functions.
Knowledge of Deloitte’s GPS practice, sectors, offerings, and market dynamics.
Understanding of products/P&L/assets is preferred
Knowledge of or experience with software licensing/assets is a plus
Experience with market research tools and practices.
Working knowledge of financial and operational metrics/KPIs (key performance indicators) used by professional services firms.
Proficiency in MS Office (including advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills) and familiarity with various research tools (Factiva, Hoovers, Bloomberg, OneSource, D&B)
Experience in financial analysis with finance based KPIs.
Demonstrated strong analytical, project management, and organizational skills.
In-depth analytical abilities, including data analysis, issue identification, and resolution tracking.
Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaborate with a team in a dynamic, cross-functional work environment.
Detail-oriented with ability to plan, prioritize, and make decisions on multiple time-sensitive projects.
Exposure to Tableau/ Power BI tools will be a value add.
Clear and concise written and verbal communication skills.


Work Location -Hyderabad.

Timings - 2 PM – 11PM

Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. title: Junior Data Analyst
description:
Role Overview

The Junior Data Consultant/Analyst is responsible for supporting end-to-end data analysis activities, from initial collection to final stakeholder reporting. This role focuses on transforming raw data into actionable business insights through advanced spreadsheet modeling and emerging data automation tools.




Key Responsibilities

Data Preparation & Cleaning: Identify, extract, and clean data from diverse sources including spreadsheets and internal databases to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Advanced Analysis: Utilize statistical techniques to detect trends, patterns, and correlations within large datasets to support organizational goals.
Consulting & Collaboration: Work with cross-functional teams to define business problems, track key performance indicators (KPIs), and recommend process improvements.
Reporting & Visualization: Develop structured reports, interactive dashboards, and visual presentations to communicate findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
System Maintenance: Assist in the upkeep and documentation of data collection systems and databases to maintain data integrity.




Technical Requirements

Advanced Excel (Must-Have): High proficiency in complex formulas (e.g., XLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH), PivotTables, and data modeling is essential for daily analysis tasks.
Preferred Tools (Pluses):
SQL: Ability to write queries to extract and manage data from relational databases.
Python: Experience with libraries like Pandas and NumPy for data manipulation and automation.
Alteryx: Hands-on experience creating automated workflows for data wrangling and ETL processes.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or Information Management.

title: Accounting Analyst
description:
Job Title: Accounting Analyst

Location: India

Department: Finance / Accounting

Position Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented Accounting Analyst to support our Workday implementation. This role will focus on validating, cleansing, reconciling, and uploading financial data into Workday to ensure accuracy during system migration and go-live. The ideal candidate has strong accounting fundamentals, ERP experience, and exceptional attention to detail.

Key Responsibilities

Workday Implementation Support


Review, validate, and reconcile financial data prior to migration into Workday
Prepare and upload data templates (e.g., GL balances, suppliers, customers, assets, projects, etc.)
Perform data cleansing and ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency
Support testing cycles (UAT, parallel runs, data validation)
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies
Maintain documentation of data mapping and reconciliation processes


Accounting & Financial Support


Reconcile general ledger balances and sub-ledgers
Assist with chart of accounts validation and mapping
Support month-end close activities during implementation
Ensure compliance with internal controls and company policies


Collaboration


Work closely with Finance, IT, and Workday implementation consultants
Coordinate with global teams across time zones
Provide status updates on data readiness and issues


Required Qualifications


3–6 years of accounting or financial systems experience
Strong understanding of:
General Ledger
Journal entries
Account reconciliations
Financial reporting
Experience working with ERP systems (Workday preferred; SAP/Oracle/NetSuite acceptable)
Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, data validation)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Strong written and verbal English communication skills

Preferred Qualifications


Prior Workday Financials experience
Experience in system implementation or data migration projects
Knowledge of internal controls and audit processes
Experience working with US-based companies or global teams


Key Competencies


Detail-oriented and highly organized
Strong data validation mindset
Ability to meet tight deadlines
Proactive communicator
Comfortable working in a structured implementation environment

title: Engineering Analyst, Trust and Safety Ads Account Entry
description:
Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
2 years of experience in data analysis, including identifying trends, generating summary statistics, and drawing insights from quantitative and qualitative data.
2 years of experience managing projects and defining project scope, goals, and deliverables.


Preferred qualifications:


Master's degree in a quantitative discipline.
2 years of experience with one or more of the following languages: SQL, R, Python, or C++.
2 years of experience with machine learning systems.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.


About The Job

Trust and Safety team members are tasked with identifying and taking on the biggest problems that challenge the safety and integrity of our products. They use technical know-how, excellent problem-solving skills, user insights, and proactive communication to protect users and our partners from abuse across Google products like Search, Maps, Gmail, and Google Ads. On this team, you're a big-picture thinker and strategic team-player with a passion for doing what’s right. You work globally and cross-functionally with Google engineers and product managers to identify and fight abuse and fraud cases at Google speed - with urgency. And you take pride in knowing that every day you are working hard to promote trust in Google and ensuring the highest levels of user safety.

In this role, you will be working with a team of Analysts that assess and address threats against Google Ads accounts in the hijacking and scaled abuse space. With your investigative, technical and security skills, you will identify and solve complex problems at scale. You will be proactive, motivated, organized, responsible, innovative and work well in a fast-paced, global, cross-functional, and team-oriented environment and demonstrate strong technical know-how, effective communication to get things done. You will also work very closely with Engineering and Product teams to make sure that you can quickly adapt our products and defenses against new threat vectors, to ensure a safe experience for users and advertisers. You will build, leverage and evaluate models and operations to solve cyber security and abuse problems.

At Google we work hard to earn our users’ trust every day. Trust & Safety is Google’s team of abuse fighting and user trust experts working daily to make the internet a safer place. We partner with teams across Google to deliver bold solutions in abuse areas such as malware, spam and account hijacking. A team of Analysts, Policy Specialists, Engineers, and Program Managers, we work to reduce risk and fight abuse across all of Google’s products, protecting our users, advertisers, and publishers across the globe in over 40 languages.

Responsibilities


Deliver actionable insights into ads account takeover and abuse threats while optimizing data collection and quality processes.
Develop early warning alerts for evolving threats by researching attacker operations and analyzing their interactions with our systems.
Lead Google Ads incident responses, including severe escalations with partner teams, while providing intelligence on adversarial campaigns and actors.
Collaborate with India-based vendors to scale ads account protection, including escalations and quality control. Deliver training and advocate for ads safety tooling enhancements.
Review graphic or offensive content and occasional on-call duties, including weekends and holidays.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

criteria.Industries: Information Services and Technology, Information and Internet

title: Business Systems Analyst, Supply Chain
description:
Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
5 years of experience designing and implementing supply chain systems.
5 years of experience building integrated systems throughout the entire life cycle (e.g., analyze, design, build, test, implement, and support).
Experience in localization and implementation of business processes for APAC regions.
Experience with eCommerce Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM), CRM, quality, or logistics.


Preferred qualifications:


Certification in APICS, CSCP or CPIM.
Experience in large-scale Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) SAP systems deployment.
Experience with master data management and working with cross-functional engineering teams.
Understanding of eCommerce order management and fulfillment ecosystem.
Ability to manage ambiguity and a rapidly changing landscape.


About The Job

At Google, we work at lightning speed. So when things get in the way of progress, the Business Systems Integration team steps in to remove those roadblocks. The team identifies time-consuming internal processes and then builds solutions that are reliable and scalable enough to work within the size and scope of the company. You understand and translate Googler needs into high-level technical specifications, design and develop recommended systems and consult with Google executives to ensure smooth implementation. Whether battling large system processes or leveraging our homegrown suite of Google products for Googlers themselves, you help Googlers work faster and more efficiently.

As a Supply Chain Business Systems Analyst, you will be architecting and providing solutions that enable supply chain functions for Google’s Devices and Services operations. Supporting a dynamic systems landscape spanning a group of tools and technologies (SAP and non-SAP systems), you will formulate recommended system design, partnering with external technology providers and internal product teams. As a domain expert, you will also influence end-to-end solution design to solve supply chain business problems using industry best practices.

The Platforms and Devices team encompasses Google's various computing software platforms across environments (desktop, mobile, applications), as well as our first party devices and services that combine the best of Google AI, software, and hardware. Teams across this area research, design, and develop new technologies to make our user's interaction with computing faster and more seamless, building innovative experiences for our users around the world.

Responsibilities


Function as the Supply Chain subject-matter-expert with a focus on order management and fulfillment processes in the eCommerce, retail or wholesale environments.
Define, design, and implement complex business solutions using custom built and third-party systems in partnership with the internal systems and supply chain business users.
Lead and participate in systems implementation projects and deliver key artifacts (e.g., requirements documentation, systems configuration, fit/gap analysis, test documentation and execution, issue identification, and resolution).
Support mission-critical and business-critical systems using IT and software engineering practices.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst
description:
Job Title: Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst

Location: Hyderabad

At Sanofi we chase the miracles of science to improve people’s lives. We are dedicated to making a positive impact on the lives of the patients and families we serve, and we accomplish our goals through world-class research and with the compassion and commitment of our employees.

As we continue to transform the practice of medicine, the next chapter of Sanofi’s Play to Win strategy will require a focus on delivering transformation and simplification of our core processes, optimizing resource allocation and deployment to fuel business growth and investment in science.

To facilitate this transformation, a new Business Operations Business Unit is being established, bringing together existing business service activities, and driving further expansion of centralized services at scale into a global unit, with a focus on driving simplification, efficiency, and productivity.

This new Business Unit will enable the delivery of best-in-class business support capabilities across the organization, incorporating and engaging disparate teams from across areas such as R&D, M&S, the Corporate Functions and GBUs into new global Service Delivery Towers with initial services spanning Commercial, Finance, Procurement and People & Culture.

Reporting to the Head of S2P Delivery, the Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst plays a critical role in managing the accounts payable end-to-end process of from invoice receiving, invoice processing to invoice payment. This position involves also the execution of month-end-activities ensuring the accurate and timely payment to suppliers while adhering to financial policies and regulatory requirements.

Responsibilities


Invoice Receiving: Ensure in collaboration with service providers and Digital team proper receiving of invoices through inbound channels.
Invoice Processing: Efficiently process invoices, verifying accuracy, and ensuring compliance with relevant policies and collaborate with internal stakeholders and BPO to resolve discrepancies and obtain necessary approvals for complex cases.
Accounts Payable Management & Month End Closing: Manage in collaboration with the BPO the account payable process, including the timely and accurate recording of financial transactions and reconcile accounts payable records with supplier statements and resolve discrepancies for complex cases.
Payment Processing: Coordinate in collaboration with the BPO and internal teams the payment process, ensuring timely disbursement to suppliers and validate payment requests against approved invoices and payment terms .
Compliance and Documentation: Ensure adherence of all processes in scope to financial policies, regulatory requirements, internal controls, and S2P Core Model and maintain organized and accurate documentation related to invoice-to-pay processes.
Issue Resolution: Address and resolve issues related to invoices, payments, and accounts payable and communicate with suppliers and internal teams to resolve payment-related queries.


Qualifications

Required:


Previous experience in accounts payable, invoice processing, or related financial roles.
Familiarity with financial systems and tools from business perspective and technical fundamentals: ERP (SAP preferred), invoice workflow tools.
Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
Transversal competencies and ability to work across processes, geographies, languages, cultures, business maturity levels.
Strong commitment to continuous process improvements
Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
Fluent in English (spoken and written).


Preferred


Knowledge of ERP systems, such as SAP.
Understanding of financial and procurement best practices.
Degree in finance, accounting, or a relevant field.

title: Financial Planning Analyst
description:
Job Title: FP&A Analyst – Sales







Role Overview




We are looking for a highly disciplined and execution-focused FP&A Analyst to independently own Sales financial reporting, budgeting, CAC tracking, incentive calculations, and projection maintenance.This is a hands-on, individual contributor role requiring strong Excel modelling skills, financial accuracy, and structured reporting discipline

This is not a strategy role. The focus is precision, governance, and execution







Core Responsibilities

1️⃣ Annual Budget & AOP Support

Prepare and maintain Annual Sales Budget and AOP sheets
Allocate budgets across regions, cost heads, and headcount plans
Maintain structured and stable financial templates







2️⃣ Monthly MIS & Cost Governance

Deliver monthly Sales MIS within defined timelines
Track revenue vs target and cost vs budget
Maintain regional performance trackers
Ensure complete reconciliation with source data







3️⃣ CAC & Cost

TrackingCalculate and publish monthly
CACMaintain fixed vs variable cost clarity
Flag ≥5% deviations from planned benchmarks
Maintain supporting documentation







4️⃣ Incentive

GovernanceExecute monthly incentive calculations (associate + manager level)
Maintain slab logic documentation
Ensure zero payout disputes due to internal calculation
errorsMaintain full audit trail







5️⃣ Projection & Trend

MonitoringUpdate rolling revenue projections
monthly Compare projection vs current trend
Flag risk deviations
≥5% vs AOPMaintain structured scenario sheets




Governing Standard




must maintain:100% calculation
accuracyFull reconciliation traceability
Zero rework due to formula or data errors




Required Competencies

Strong financial modelling skills in Excel
Google SheetsHigh-precision execution discipline
Experience handling sales-linked
financial models Incentive payout execution
experienceStrong data reconciliation and version control discipline
Exposure to sales cost structures and CAC calculations
Ability to work independently
Comfortable presenting numbers to senhout supervision










Ideal Candidate Background







3–5 years of relevant experience in Sales Finance / Revenue MIS / FP&A (execution-focused)
Individual contributor (no team management exposure required)
Primary preference - EdTech experience (sales -heavy environment)
Secondary preference: NBFC / FinTech with sales incentive exposure
Average tenure ~2 years per company (startup exposure acceptable)
Proven exposure to large sales teams and incentive-driven structures

title: Financial Analyst I
description:
Description

GAR (Global Accts Rec) is looking for a proactive, customer and people centric Finance Team Leader to support the Collections process. The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing the collection efforts, ensuring that accounts are handled appropriately and communicate with clients as required to increase the percentage of on-time payments. This person is result-oriented, multitasker with strong communication skills. Foster team unity as you build relationships while working closely with your team and fellow managers to continually reach department goals

Key job responsibilities


Be an expert in the collection processes.
Support creation and implementation of strategies to increase the number of successful collections on past due invoices and increase the penetration of accounts
Handle communication with clients on complex scenarios to ensure good customer experience
Collect, track and present metrics, and drive process improvements
Troubleshoot and triage any technical issues during collections and report as needed for long term sustainability of the process
Contribute to improvements in collection projects by identifying any issues, proposing enhancements in tools and working on continuous improvement of collection protocol and process.
Communicate internally or externally to identify issues and propose solutions for long term sustainability of processes
Willing to create and implement standard operating procedures, quality guidelines and process workflows
Leading and developing a team of collection analysts, including hiring, training, and employee development
Managing group workload , establishing priorities and performing regular audits to ensure existing policies and procedures are followed
Managing annual performance targets, creating and monitoring appropriate performance metrics
Act as an escalation point in resolving complex outstanding issues with internal and external stakeholders


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or above in finance, accounting or related field
In depth knowledge of Order to Cash Process
6+ years of experience in Accounts Receivables with at least 1 year of people management and collections experience
Demonstrate ability to lead diverse talent within a team, work cross-functionally, and build consensus on difficult issues
Excellent negotiation, interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Ability to work under pressure and with strict deadlines
Proficient in Microsoft Excel and Word
Experience in driving process improvements and operational efficiency projects


Preferred Qualifications


Post-graduation or a Master’s Degree Accounting/Finance or a related field

title: Analyst - Treasury - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

USI Financial Reporting – Treasury Analyst The Treasury Analyst will be instrumental in executing day-to-day treasury activities, supporting effective fund management, accurate financial reporting, and adherence to applicable regulatory requirements. The role partners closely with banking contacts, internal stakeholders, and auditors, and needs strong attention to detail, proficiency with SAP and online banking platforms and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Work you’ll do Responsible for supporting treasury operations, banking transactions, reconciliations, payments, and compliance including: a) Manage end-to-end banking transactions and ensure timely recording of all receipts and payments. b) Prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) on a daily/weekly/monthly basis and ensure completeness and accuracy of reconciliations. c) Identify, investigate, and resolve reconciliation variances between bank statements and internal accounting records. d) Review banking transactions for compliance and accuracy and coordinate payment approvals with authorized signatories. e) Maintain and track an authorization log for transactions requiring approvals, including status and closure. f) Manage treasury banking activities including term deposits (maturity/interest tracking), bank guarantees (issuance/renewal), and regulated payments. g) Process auto-BRS for prior-day transactions and post manual reconciliation entries as required (including intern payments). h) Generate and maintain UTR details for GST Input Tax Credit (ITC) and share information with the Tax team when required. i) Monitor and manage the treasury mailbox, responding to queries and ensuring timely action on requests. j) Prepare payment upload files, process payments, and handle NEFT rejects by coordinating with stakeholders and clearing entries in SAP (F-03). k) Prepare Form 15CA documentation, upload payments on the bank portal, and coordinate with auditors for Form 15CA/15CB filing while maintaining audit-ready records. l) Prepare cash forecasts and budgets for leadership review, support hedging inputs, liaise with banks on transaction issues, reconcile bank-related balances (including bank tax deductions), and support payroll uploads/validations and salary cost reclassifications. The team Deloitte’s Finance team provides enterprise financial stewardship, delivering accurate, timely reporting and insights that inform strategic decision-making. We uphold strong governance, controls, and compliance while enabling performance management through planning, forecasting and analytics across the firm. Our focus is to strengthen financial integrity and transparency while improving efficiency through standardization and automation. Qualifications


Required: Chartered Accountant (CA)/CPA/CMA with 0 to 2 years of experience Work Location: Hyderabad Timing: 11 AM- 8PM


Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

title: Associate Financial Analyst
description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The CFO organization comprises a broad group of finance disciplines, with the common goal of partnering with business teams delivering strategies and providing our players with amazing experiences. Our finance teams assist the business in achieving profitable creativity by proving financial insight, reporting and guidance.

We use progressive tools to analyze and visualize data, telling the story of financial drivers, performance, risks and opportunities. We value attributes like collaboration and learning. We celebrate diversity and inclusion by creating a place where you can come to work and be yourself.

Location: Hyderabad

You will report to Senior Financial Analyst, FP&A

Responsibilities


Responsible for preparing and reporting actual financial performance, comparing it with budgeted figures and the latest forecast. Also responsible for handling estimate reporting comparing it with the prior estimate and plan scenarios.
Collect and consolidate financial data from various sources, ensuring accuracy and completeness, to perform in-depth analysis on daily ad hoc finance requests.
Develop and deliver new financial reports or insights based on business team requests and evolving information needs.
Maintain regular communication with finance partners across multiple global locations to ensure seamless coordination and alignment.


Qualifications


Academic Qualifications - Good to have Master of Business Administration in Finance but not mandatory. Bachelor’s degree required – Finance / Accounting related field preferred.
0 - 2 years of related Financial and FP&A experience
Demonstrated passion for learning and growing.
Highly motived with an ability to manage multiple and sometimes competing priorities.
Comfortable building analysis based off data from multiple sources.
Extreme attention to detail with a rigorous process for review of accuracy.
Excellent oral / written communication and interpersonal skills that enable clear and concise communication to all levels of the organization.
Adept in MS Office, with an emphasis on Excel and PowerPoint.
Experience with Anaplan and / Power Bi is a plus


About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

title: Business Analyst
description:
About the Role:

This individual will provide backend office support, data analysis & visualization and reporting assistance to the Business Head. The position involves data collection, report preparation, coordination with sales and field teams, and support in in driving business through analytics in different domains viz sales, competition, mapping, cost benefit analysis, productivity - derived from system.

Key Responsibilities:

Collect data from sales teams, branches, and internal departments for ongoing projects and reports.
Coordinate with team for timely completion of assignments given by Business Head, taking required reports on or before time, and update Business Head
Proactively engage with Business Head and team for preparing annual Business plan / sales forecast
To analyze future business plan (sales, marketing, competition, benchmark, business forecast, future challenges etc.) as part of Strategic Business reviews at Management level.
Deep analysis of success and failures in adherence to the targets & to monitor projects & their progress by tracking status of activities, resolving problems, generating progress reports, recommending actions in consultation with the Business Head
Analyze investment v/s benefit of various Market development / Marketing Activities undertaken by the company for bringing more focus on time bound results.
Consolidate data from multiple sources and prepare analytical reports to highlight gaps and progress.
Coordinate with regional and branch teams for status updates and follow-ups on ongoing initiatives.
Help plan and organize meetings, prepare presentations, and record Minutes of Meeting (MoM).
Maintain trackers, reports, and documentation in a structured manner.
Support preparation of management presentations using Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.

.Key Competencies

Working knowledge of MS Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Sales force, MIS and SAP.
Proficient in ppt preparation, analytical and reporting skills.
Good coordination and follow-up abilities.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet timelines.
Experience in using Analytics for data-based decision making
Opportunity to work with a well-established brand
Strong communication and interpersonal skills




Qualifications and Experience

Education:
B. Tech / Management graduate; Master’s degree preferred.
Experience:
5–7 years of experience in reporting, coordination, backend support, or similar roles.

title: Data Analyst
description:
Role Purpose

To support the secured lending business by developing, managing, and optimizing MIS reports, dashboards, and performance trackers. The role will ensure timely and accurate data availability for decision-making across sales, credit, collections, and product teams.

Track business KPIs and SLAs across sales, credit, and operations.

Assist in preparing dashboards and presentations for leadership reviews.

title: Research Analyst
description:
Job Purpose

To recommend and advise investors for various investment ideas from medium to long term perspective by doing quantitative and qualitative analysis of listed companies. To contribute to the team activities in generating investment ideas, creating and maintaining large and complex databases, help in preparing various reports like strategy note, morning note, weekly market updates. These activities will help in driving sales and information dissemination to investors and sales force.

Job Context & Major Challenges

Aditya Birla Money Ltd. (ABML) is primarily a Broking Company, deals in Equity, Commodity & Currency. Workforce of ~800 spread across ~40 locations with customer base of in excess of 3 lacs. Commodity operations are managed through its 100% subsidiary Aditya Birla Commodities Broking limited. ABML is one of the leading securities trading companies in the country. ABML operates in a fragmented equity broking business and has a daily turnover of close to 3,000 Crores with a market share of 1%. ABM works in a highly regulated environment with a host of regulators like SEBI, RBI, AMFI, FMC, IRDA etc. regulating its day to day operations. In addition to the regulator ABML is also subject to close monitoring and inspection through various agencies like NSE / BSE / NCDX / MCX / NSDL / CDSL etc. ABML also operates in a highly competitive market mainly in fragmented industry with ABML competition from major National Broking houses like ICICI Securities, HDFC Securities, Angel Broking, MOFSL etc. and a range of local Broking outfits.

Majority of the broking houses have set up in-house research team that scans companies and stocks as well as analyse macro-economic scenario that impacts the stock market. The Research team at the Fundamental side values the securities based on the growth prospects and attractiveness of the underlying business. It involves analyzing the financials, competitive advantages of the business, quality of management and competitive environment it operates in among other factors. The theory of Research revolves around the fact that the market price of an asset may deviate from its fair value in short term due to extraneous factors such as market trends or investor sentiments, but will tend to move towards its intrinsic value over the long term.

The Key Responsibilities Of Sr. /Research Analyst Are


To build large databases by extracting and collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc.
To analyse large and complex databases of macro and micro information and create well-structured reports.
To disseminate research reports/output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions.
To provide research support and inputs for consumption by the investment team in building portfolios and prepare reports/advisory for the clients.
To timely work on and generate investment ideas so as to capture maximum gains out of it.


The Primary Challenges Faced Are


To find investible ideas based on rigorous analysis of data/information sourced from quality database available in the market within a sector, given different risk return profiles and competitive advantages of various companies within a sector.
In a an environment, which is constantly moving and changing, the key challenge is to stay abreast with latest updates in a sector and the companies within it, so as to take nimble investment decisions.
The challenge is to clearly communicate the investment thesis for a company/sector in a manner which clearly highlights the investment case and arguments so that the investment team/investors can take an informed investment decision.
Another key challenge of the role is to take relative investment calls when absolute opportunities are more than what can be accommodated in the portfolio


Critical Skillsets are the individual must have a strong knack for financial analysis and equity research. The role involves minor level of client interaction and hence good interpersonal skills are desirable.

Key Result Areas

KRA (Accountabilities) (Max 1325 Characters)Supporting Actions (Max 1325 Characters)

KRA1 To be able to build, maintain and analyze large and complex databases of macro and micro information


To build large databases by extracting/collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc. and organize the data into sub-heads (viz. macros / micros / sectoral / valuations).
To understand the growth trends of the industry by analysing parameters affecting the dynamics of the industry.
To analyze data using top-down approach (international asset class level, country level and sector level) and also on a bottom up basis on assigned sectors to come up with investment ideas for the investment team and clients.


KRA2 To be able to conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of the companies of specific sectors and participate in team research activities


To analyze companies/sectors using tools like ratio analysis, time series and cross sectional analysis, so as to make a judgment regarding investment potential of the various sectors and the companies within various sectors.
To read relevant materials from the industry body and participate in industry conferences to maintain the sharpness in investment analysis.
To meet the managements of a company and understand the key trends in the sector, the company and its future plan.
To project the earnings of the company in medium and long term.


KRA3 To be able to disseminate research based output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions


To prepare an investment argument based on the findings of the analysis and help the investment team build a portfolio as per the risk/return framework of the investor.
To contribute to various products which require team efforts like strategic investment report, morning report, morning sales note, concalls, weekly report.
To increase the visibility of the research team by contributing articles to various media reports.

title: Commercial Business Analyst
description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU'LL DO



As Commercial Business Analyst, you are responsible for driving the strategic insight work and analysis of our merchandising through advanced data, customer and consumer insights in regard to our product. You will independently be able to set the scope and initiate analysis deepening on the need of the merchandising organization, demonstrate leadership, analytical prowess, and a deep understanding of industry trends.​



​You have a deep interest in retail analytics, inventory optimization and merchandising strategy development. You are customer and sales-driven professional with a strategic mindset. You thrive working in a fast pace, in and agile environment. You are natural team player that strives to foster collaboration across channels, roles and functions.​



Key responsibilities​



Conduct in-depth analysis of sales trends, customer behaviors and market dynamics to identify growth opportunities and areas for improvement; using statistical models and forecasting techniques to drive data driven decision-making.​



You stay abreast of industry trends, competitor product strategies, and emerging technologies to challenge current plans, maintain competitive advantage and support executional excellence by supporting with market intelligence in how to position, activate and optimize our offers.​



Analyze (and align with Customer Activation) SEO insights to support the SEO with our assortment strategy in close collaboration with merchandising managers / ecommerce leads as well as drive insights to support assortment strategy for all our channels; retail, digital and marketplaces.​



You drive projects and build business cases for new product categories, sales channels and other business opportunities to support new sales and profit growth.​



Follow up on our full (omni, retail and ecom) commercial initiatives and build strong case for improvement in our discount strategy, leveraging web analytics, customer insights, sales and cost data; enabling best possible commercial decision.



Please click here for complete role description



​WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH



You will collaborate with the colleagues from Merchandising team.



WHO YOU ARE​



To be successful in the role as Commercial Business Analyst, you should have strong commercial focus and are insight and data driven, with proven ability to drive results and work towards goals. You should have a broad merchandising knowledge and commercial experience that understands the total picture from customer demands on our offers.​



​What you need to succeed:​



Bachelor's degree, preferably in Economics or Business degree/relevant qualifications.​



Strong in driving commercial product excellence, strategic and analytical, with deep experience in business case building as well as follow up and estimate performance.​



Understanding of product, stocks and lifecycle management.​



High level of salesmanship and analytical proficiency; is an advance excel user.​



Effective collaborator with strong stakeholder management and communication skills.



Knowledge & experience in the Indian fashion retail.



WHO WE ARE​​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ ​



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​ ​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​​



At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​​



You will receive 25% off H&M Group brands; H&M, COS, Monki, Weekdays, Arket, & Other Stories
We are an inclusive company where you are encouraged to be yourself at work


Additional Information



This is a full-time position, reporting to Regional Head of Merchandising.



Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible, but no later than April 24th, 2026. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through career page.

title: Data Analyst
description:
A data analyst is responsible for organizing data related to sales numbers, market research, logistics, linguistics, or other behaviours. They utilize technical expertise to ensure data is accurate and high-quality. Data is then analyzed, designed, and presented in a way that assists individuals, businesses, and organizations make better decisions.

Using automated tools to extract data from primary and secondary sources

Removing corrupted data and fixing coding errors and related problems

Developing and maintaining databases, data systems – reorganizing data in a readable format

Performing analysis to assess quality and meaning of data

Filter Data by reviewing reports and performance indicators to identify and correct code problems

Using statistical tools to identify, analyze, and interpret patterns and trends in complex data sets that could be helpful for the diagnosis and prediction

Assigning numerical value to essential business functions so that business performance can be assessed and compared over periods of time.

Analyzing local, national, and global trends that impact both the organization and the industry

Preparing reports for the management stating trends, patterns, and predictions using relevant data

Working with programmers, engineers, and management heads to identify process improvement opportunities, propose system modifications, and devise data governance strategies.

Preparing final analysis reports for the stakeholders to understand the data-analysis steps, enabling them to take important decisions based on various facts and trends.

title: Business Analyst - Service Central Operations
description:
About Us:

Paytm is India’s leading mobile payments and financial services distribution company, and a pioneer of the QR payments revolution. We build technologies that empower small businesses and drive financial inclusion at scale. Our mission is to bring half a billion Indians into the mainstream economy through the power of technology.




About the Role:

We are looking for a strategic Business Analyst who can go beyond data reporting to influence product and business decisions. This role sits at the intersection of data, product, and strategy—focused on uncovering insights, shaping roadmaps, and driving measurable business impact. You will work closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to define problems, evaluate opportunities, and enable data-backed decision-making at scale.




Key Responsibilities:

Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and strategic recommendations for senior stakeholders
Define, track, and evolve key business and product KPIs aligned with organizational goals
Identify whitespace opportunities and contribute to the development of new product features, business models, and growth strategies
Track and analyze ticket reopen rates and false closures to identify gaps in resolution quality and process adherence
Design and run structured merchant dip surveys post-closure to capture feedback, validate resolution effectiveness, and uncover experience gaps
Identify recurring patterns across tickets, agent behavior, and issue categories; flag systemic problems and drive cross-functional resolution
Build and own quality dashboards and root cause analysis (RCA) packs to enable data-driven decision-making for operations and leadership
Partner with Operations, Product, and Training teams to translate insights into process improvements, SOP enhancements, and agent coaching interventions




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field
3 – 5 years of experience in analytics, consulting, strategy, or product-focused roles
Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to connect data insights to business outcomes
Hands-on experience in SQL and working with large datasets (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Hive, etc.)
Experience with data visualization tools such as Looker or Google Data Studio
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis/modeling is a plus
Familiarity with experimentation frameworks, statistical analysis, and predictive modeling
Strong communication skills with the ability to influence stakeholders across functions




Why Join Us:

At Paytm, you won’t just analyze data—you’ll shape decisions that impact millions of users and businesses across India. Our culture is driven by ownership, speed, and a relentless focus on customers. As the largest merchant acquirer in the country, we offer a unique opportunity to work on high-scale, high-impact problems at the forefront of India’s digital economy.

title: Business Analyst
description:
Organization Unit Purpose




Group Transaction Banking, which includes Core Trade, Supply Chain & Receivable financing, Escrow and Cash Management and Merchant ACquiring. Transaction banking has been identified as one of the growth areas within ENBD Group. Transaction banking business coverage is across all international branches. The Group focus is to develop capabilities in Transaction banking across the group and generate fee income. Transaction banking values customer relationships and works closely with our clients to help them route their Transaction banking business with ENBD. Transaction banking business focus is delivery of innovative and world class products through our channels such as web/ mobile/ H2H/ Swift amongst others.




Job Purpose




Merchant Acquiring Business Analyst is responsible for analyzing business requirements, defining functional specifications, and supporting the implementation of acquiring solutions across POS and E-commerce channels. The role ensures business objectives are clearly translated into system capabilities while maintaining operational efficiency, compliance, and customer satisfaction.







Strategic:

• Gather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements for merchant acquiring processes.

• Conduct process analysis and gap assessments to identify improvements in acquiring workflows and system functionalities.

• Define end-to-end process flows covering merchant onboarding, transaction processing, settlement, reconciliation, dispute handling, and reporting.

• Collaborate with Product, Business, Operations, Risk, IT, Compliance, and Finance teams to ensure alignment on system requirements and business priorities.

• Support the design of new product features and enhancements within the acquiring ecosystem.

• Assist in developing operational procedures, documentation, and training materials for internal stakeholders.

• Ensure all processes and solutions comply with regulatory, risk, and card scheme requirements.

• Provide analytical insights and recommendations to enhance product performance and operational efficiency.







Ongoing:




• Be the decision making member of senior representatives from business and IT in categorizing the migration change management tasks into Agile and Platform streams.

• In collaboration with the Product Owners, manage scope and deliver the program scope as agreed/committed in the program, balancing pragmatism with core product functionality, without compromising the original intent of the product

• Be responsible for creating User Training plans, manuals and training methodologies

• Own the business user acceptance testing and build a core business test team around the migration extraction, validations, and reconciliation tasks.

• Own the end to end change management process for which includes, BRD, Product Development, Testing and rollout

• Be responsible for resolving day to day issues raised by various stakeholders, by putting up a robust production support and management structure including both business support teams and IT support teams.







Job Content




Customers / Stakeholders

Manage Projects with Schemes
Keep track of deliverables
Create BRD’s for merchant acquiring platform , payment gateways and POS
Work with technology and business teams and ensure the product is developed as per requirements
Support product team in User acceptance testing




Relationship Management

Deputizing for Product Owner in relevant forums
Proactively manage internal stakeholders and take personal responsibility for the establishment and maintenance of key relationships to leverage business achievement. This includes IT, RISK, Legal & all TBS product business units.




Resource Management

Ensure provision, deployment and optimal utilization of assigned resources in order to achieve efficiencies, cost effectiveness and productivity




Risk Management/Operative effectiveness & Controls

To deliver product and services that fully meet internal control measures and which comply fully with legal and compliance and regulatory requirements.
To continually reassess the operational risks associated with the role and inherent in the business, Taking account of changing economic or market conditions, legal and regulatory requirements, operating procedures and practices, management restructurings and the impact of new technology.
In conjunction with the Compliance department, to implement the Group compliance policy. This will include all relevant financial services laws, rules and codes with which the business has to comply.




Education




BE/BTech
BCom with relevant Transaction Banking / Banking /Technology experience




Experiences




Minimum 2 years of experience in Merchant Acquiring Business
Understanding of Payment Gateways
Strong knowledge of merchant acquiring lifecycle – onboarding, transaction processing, settlement, dispute management, and reconciliation.
Understanding of card and digital payment flows, including authorization, clearing, and settlement cycles.




Knowledge & Skills




Excellent communication skills written and verbal.
Confident in presentation to senior stakeholders.
Technology skillsets related to Transaction Banking systems and banking systems like Core, Channels, Middleware is an added advantage.




Behavioral Competencies




Agile Maturity
Proactive and a Team Player
Business focused
Professional Expertise
Market Knowledge & Networking
Client Management

title: Senior Product Analyst/Product Analyst
description:
Job Title: Senior Product Analyst – Notifications, Email & Retention

Department: Product / Growth

Experience Required: Minimum 3 Years

Location: Gurgaon




About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Product Analyst to drive data-led decision making across our Notifications, Email, and Retention initiatives. This role will partner closely with Product Managers, Growth, Marketing, and Engineering teams to analyze user behavior, optimize engagement channels, and improve customer retention across the ecommerce lifecycle.

The ideal candidate combines strong analytical skills, deep SQL expertise, business understanding, and an AI-first mindset to unlock insights and scale personalization across customer communication channels.




Key Responsibilities

Own analytics for customer engagement channels including Push Notifications, Email, and lifecycle communications.
Analyze user behavior across the ecommerce funnel to identify opportunities to improve retention, repeat purchases, and engagement.
Build and maintain SQL-based data models, dashboards, and reports to track key product and retention metrics.
Design and analyze A/B tests and experiments for notifications, email campaigns, and personalization strategies.
Partner with Product Managers to define success metrics and evaluate feature performance.
Develop insights on user cohorts, segmentation, and lifecycle behavior to improve CRM and engagement strategies.
Work with marketing and growth teams to optimize campaign targeting, frequency, and messaging effectiveness.
Leverage AI tools and AI-first thinking to automate analysis, generate insights faster, and improve personalization strategies.
Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders and influence product roadmap decisions.




Required Skills

SQL (Advanced): Strong ability to query large datasets and build analytical models.
Excel / Google Sheets: Advanced data manipulation, analysis, and reporting.
AI-First Thinking: Experience using AI tools for data exploration, analysis automation, and productivity.
Strong understanding of product analytics, experimentation, and user lifecycle metrics.
Experience with cohort analysis, funnel analysis, and retention metrics.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate data insights into business recommendations.




Preferred Skills

Experience with product analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, GA4, etc.).
Experience working with CRM platforms or marketing automation tools.
Familiarity with Python or data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.).
Experience in ecommerce or marketplace businesses.




Key Metrics You Will Impact

Customer Retention Rate
Repeat Purchase Rate
Notification / Email CTR & Conversion
User Engagement & Lifecycle Activation




Why Join Us

Opportunity to work on high-impact growth and retention problems in a fast-scaling ecommerce platform.
Collaborate with product, growth, and marketing teams to drive measurable business outcomes.
Build AI-enabled analytics capabilities that power smarter product decisions.




Reporting To: Product / Growth Leadership

Employment Type: Full-Time

title: Business Analyst
description:
Main Purpose:

A business analyst (BA) bridges the gap between business needs and technical solutions. They analyze business problems, propose solutions, and ensure those solutions effectively meet the needs of the organization. BAs work across various sectors and projects, helping businesses improve efficiency, increase profitability, and achieve their strategic goals

Knowledge Skills and Abilities, Key Responsibilities:


Problem Identification and Analysis:
Identify and analyze business problems, understand their root causes, and assess their impact on the organization.

Solution Development:
Develop and propose solutions to address identified problems, considering various factors like feasibility, cost, and potential benefits.

Requirements Gathering and Management:
Gather and document business requirements, ensuring they are clear and concise

Stakeholder Communication:
Communicate effectively with stakeholders, including business users, technical teams, and management, to ensure everyone is informed and aligned.

Project Support:
Support implementation of solutions, monitoring progress, managing risks, and ensuring the project stays on track.

Process Improvement:
Analyze existing business processes, identify areas for improvement, and recommend changes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness.



Skills Required for a Business Analyst:


Analytical Skills: Strong analytical skills to understand complex business problems and data.
Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to interact with diverse stakeholders.
Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to identify problems, analyze their causes, and develop effective solutions.
Technical Skills: Significant understanding of technologies and systems.
Stakeholder Management: Ability to manage and influence stakeholders with different perspectives.
Business Acumen: Understanding of business principles and practices.
Project Management: Knowledge of project management methodologies and tools.



Key Relationships and Department Overview:

Internal & External Stakeholders

title: Business Analyst
description:
Description

Are you customer obsessed, flexible, smart and analytical, strategic yet execution focused and passionate about e-commerce? Are you an experienced, entrepreneurial leader with a strong work ethic? If yes, this opportunity will appeal to you.

Amazon is looking for a Business Analyst for Amazon Business.

We are looking for customer obsessed, data driven entrepreneurs to join our growing team. Solve some of the hardest problems for our customers. If you want operate at start up speed, solve some of the hardest problems and build a service which customers love, Amazon.in might just be the place for you.

The Business Analyst is responsible for driving deep insights about Amazon Business and driving continuous improvement using the analysis. The person should have a detailed understanding of a business requirement or the ability to quickly get to the root cause of a particular business issue, and draft solutions to meet requirements or resolve the root problems.

Some of the key result areas include, but not limited to:


Own the design, development, and maintenance of ongoing metrics, reports, analyses, dashboards, etc. to drive key business decisions. Ensure data accuracy by validating data for new and existing tools. Learn and understand a broad range of Amazon’s data resources and know how, when, and which to use and which not to use.
Work closely with category, marketing and sales stakeholders to understand and automate processes
Understand trends related to Amazon business and recommend strategies to stakeholders to help drive business growth. Respond with urgency to high priority requests from senior business leaders.
Identify, develop, manage, and execute analyses to uncover areas of opportunity and present written business recommendations that will help shape the direction of the business.
The ideal candidate actively seeks to understand Amazon’s core business values and initiatives, and translates those into everyday practices.


Basic Qualifications


1+ years of data analytics or automation experience
1+ years of capacity planning, operations planning, business analysis or similar experience
Bachelor's degree
Knowledge of data pipelining and extraction using SQL
Knowledge of SQL and Excel at a moderate or advanced level


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in capacity planning, operations planning, business analysis or similar


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.


Company - ASSPL - Karnataka - B56
